archetype: ARCHETYPE-2 (Integration) — @netscript/queue is a multi-adapter package wrapping external systems (Deno.Kv, Postgres, Redis, AMQP) behind package-owned ports; adding a new DeadLetterStorePort + named KvDeadLetterStore adapter is squarely the port+adapter integration archetype.
scopeOverlays: none — packages/queue is a library; no SCOPE-service (no service main.ts), no SCOPE-frontend, no SCOPE-docs file authoring beyond the package README that ships with the unit
lockedDecisions: Introduce package-owned port `DeadLetterStorePort<T>` in packages/queue/ports/dead-letter.ts with a structured `DeadLetterRecord<T>` (reason discriminant aligned to doctrine 08 NormalizedError), minimal ops append/list/reprocess/depth (AP-3 avoidance).

Default durable store = `KvDeadLetterStore` wrapping injected `@netscript/kv` WatchableKv, reusing KvPolling's proven `['queue:dlq', queueName, failedAt, messageId]` key layout; KV resolved lazily on first use, never at module load (AP-11).

Provider-specific durable stores: `PostgresDeadLetterStore` (new `<table>_dlq` table via the adapter's existing PostgresQueueClient) and `RedisDeadLetterStore` (Redis LIST `netscript:dlq:<queue>` via existing commands client).

Each adapter gets `deadLetterStore?` constructor option + private `#dlq` with a provider-appropriate default; nack({requeue:false}) and max-attempts route to `#dlq.append` BEFORE the existing discard/DELETE.
Refactor KvPolling DLQ (write/depth/reprocess) onto the same port so the package has ONE DLQ contract; behavior preserved byte-for-byte via default KvDeadLetterStore.

DenoKv and AMQP no-op nack paths replaced with real nack that durably dead-letters via KvDeadLetterStore (always-available sink); RabbitMQ DLX documented as optional higher-throughput path, not the guaranteed path.

Add `QueueOptions.deadLetterStore?` threaded through create-queue factory; typed-queue `onValidationError:'dlq'` now reliably dead-letters with reason 'validation_failed' on every adapter (no public enum change).
No new dependency and no catalog/version-pin change — @netscript/kv already imported; @fedify/* pins untouched.
testPlan: Unit: KvDeadLetterStore append→list→depth→reprocess against in-test Deno.Kv (--unstable-kv); reprocess returns count and clears entries.
Unit: PostgresDeadLetterStore against an in-memory fake PostgresQueueClient — append INSERT…ON CONFLICT idempotent, list/depth/reprocess SQL shape.
Unit: RedisDeadLetterStore against a fake commands client — rpush/lrange/llen/lpop semantics.
Failure-path: Postgres adapter nack({requeue:false}) writes a DeadLetterRecord(reason:'nack_without_requeue') to the store before DELETE; max-attempts path writes reason:'max_attempts_exceeded'.
Failure-path: Redis adapter nack({requeue:false}) appends to DLQ store instead of dropping (assert depth==1, processing list emptied).
Failure-path: DenoKv + AMQP adapters nack({requeue:false}) durably append (no longer no-ops).
Regression: KvPolling DLQ behavior preserved after refactor — getStats().dlq, reprocessDlq, max-attempts routing unchanged.
Integration: createTypedQueue(..., {onValidationError:'dlq'}) over a default adapter — invalid message produces a DeadLetterRecord(reason:'validation_failed').
Port-contract: MemoryQueueAdapter honors nack({requeue:false}) into an injected in-memory store.
planGateSelfCheck: "Research present and current: PASS — every cited file:line re-verified on HEAD cc3b8731; gap confirmed unresolved, none dropped. Decisions locked: PASS — port shape, default + provider stores, per-adapter wiring, factory threading all named with rationale. Open-decision sweep: PASS — only two decisions (reprocess concurrency = safe-to-defer additive; AMQP DLX vs KV = resolved-now to KV sink); neither forces rework if deferred. Commit slices: PASS — 8 ordered slices <30, each names proof + gate + files. Risk register: PASS. Gate set selected: PASS — ARCHETYPE-2 F-1/F-3/F-5/F-6/F-7/F-8/F-10/F-12/F-14/F-15 + static + targeted tests + publish dry-run; no e2e:cli (no scaffold change). Deferred scope explicit: PASS. jsr-audit: PASS — applied to full export map, slow-type risk named + mitigated with explicit return annotations. Residual FAIL_PLAN risk: low — only if PLAN-EVAL wants RabbitMQ broker-side DLX as the guaranteed AMQP path; mitigated by locking KV sink as guaranteed and documenting DLX as optional."
openQuestions: Reprocess concurrency is sequential by default; a bounded-parallel reprocess option is deferred (additive, no rework).

Whether PLAN-EVAL prefers RabbitMQ broker-side dead-letter-exchange as the AMQP guaranteed path instead of the KV sink — plan locks KV sink as guaranteed and documents DLX as optional; can be revisited if evaluator insists.
risks: Refactoring KvPolling's working DLQ onto the new port could regress proven behavior — mitigated by a default KvDeadLetterStore that preserves the exact key layout + a regression test before/after.

Postgres/Redis DLQ adds a write before discard; a store-append failure must not silently drop the message — mitigated by appending before ack/DELETE and propagating store errors as QueueError so the message stays claimed for redelivery.
AMQP/DenoKv delivery is Fedify-owned; adding a NetScript nack DLQ path must not double-handle — mitigated by only dead-lettering on explicit nack({requeue:false}), leaving Fedify retry untouched.
New public exports risk JSR slow-types — mitigated by explicit return-type annotations and full-export-map jsr-audit.
Scope creep into idempotency/applied-keys (sibling slices) — explicitly out of scope; DLQ records carry messageId only, no dedup semantics added here.
